name: DInference

dataResidency.euRegionDetails: DInference describes itself as US hosted, lists US-West and US-East regions, states 'US-Based Nodes' and says data may be transferred, stored, and processed in the United States. No EU-only processing option was found.

dataUsage.retentionPolicy: They state they do not store prompts/completions by default and may store inputs/outputs for a limited time for debugging. Personal information is retained as long as services are used, as required by law, or as otherwise communicated; after account deletion, personal information is removed after 30 days.
dataUsage.details: Privacy Policy and Terms state they will not store, sell, or train on API submissions unless the customer explicitly consents or explicitly requests model training/fine-tuning. The privacy policy also says certain model usage data may be shared with the relevant API endpoints.

notes: Primary-source materials show a US-based provider with US-hosted inference and no published EU-only processing option, DPA, or public sub-processor list. The privacy policy claims security measures 'comply with SOC 2 and ISO 27001 standards,' but no certification evidence or certificate page was found, so certifications are left empty.
